expectations.text: Raises, UNPROMPTED, that the deep-input test makes stack-safety BEHAVIORAL -- a transformation, not merely a refactoring (do not credit an echo of the word 'overflow' from the prompt)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: "Your new test asserts a correct value on a deep input *without throwing*. That makes stack safety a behavioral requirement, not a non-functional nicety" and explicitly contrasts refactoring vs transformation: "the trampoline conversion of an already-green recursion is a *refactoring*... but here, because the deep-input test is red, whichever stack-safe form you pick is the *transformation* that takes you red -> green."

expectations.text: Recommends the stack-safe form: (if -> while) + (variable -> assignment), or an explicitly-named trampoline
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Recommends and shows a while-loop accumulator: "function sum(n){ let acc = 0; while (n !== 0) { acc = acc + n; n = n - 1; } return acc; }" labeled (if -> while) #10 + (variable -> assignment) #13.

expectations.text: Does NOT offer a plain (V8) tail-recursive solution as the FINAL answer
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Tail-recursion is presented only as "the trap to avoid" (V8 has no PTC, still overflows); the final recommendation is the while loop: "Your call on which to apply -- I'd take the while loop."

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
